2603.16822.title: Surg$Σ$: A Spectrum of Large-Scale Multimodal Data and Foundation Models for Surgical Intelligence
2603.16822.paper_summary: Surgical intelligence has the potential to improve the safety and consistency of surgical care, yet most existing surgical AI frameworks remain task-specific and struggle to generalize across procedures and institutions. Although multimodal foundation models, particularly multimodal large language models, have demonstrated strong cross-task capabilities across various medical domains, their advancement in surgery remains constrained by the lack of large-scale, systematically curated multimodal data. To address this challenge, we introduce Surg$Σ$, a spectrum of large-scale multimodal data and foundation models for surgical intelligence. At the core of this framework lies Surg$Σ$-DB, a large-scale multimodal data foundation designed to support diverse surgical tasks. Surg$Σ$-DB consolidates heterogeneous surgical data sources (including open-source datasets, curated in-house clinical collections and web-source data) into a unified schema, aiming to improve label consistency and data standardization across heterogeneous datasets. Surg$Σ$-DB spans 6 clinical specialties and diverse surgical types, providing rich image- and video-level annotations across 18 practical surgical tasks covering understanding, reasoning, planning, and generation, at an unprecedented scale (over 5.98M conversations). Beyond conventional multimodal conversations, Surg$Σ$-DB incorporates hierarchical reasoning annotations, providing richer semantic cues to support deeper contextual understanding in complex surgical scenarios. We further provide empirical evidence through recently developed surgical foundation models built upon Surg$Σ$-DB, illustrating the practical benefits of large-scale multimodal annotations, unified semantic design, and structured reasoning annotations for improving cross-task generalization and interpretability.
2603.16822.paper_summary_zh: 手術智能有潛力改善手術護理的安全性和一致性，然而大多數現有的手術人工智慧框架仍然是特定任務的，並且難以在不同的程序和機構之間進行泛化。儘管多模態基礎模型，特別是多模態大型語言模型，在各種醫療領域展示了強大的跨任務能力，但它們在手術領域的進展仍受到缺乏大規模、系統性策劃的多模態數據的限制。為了解決這一挑戰，我們推出了Surg$Σ$，這是一個大型多模態數據和手術智能基礎模型的範疇。該框架的核心是Surg$Σ$-DB，一個旨在支持多樣化手術任務的大型多模態數據基礎。Surg$Σ$-DB將異質的手術數據來源（包括開源數據集、策劃的內部臨床收集和網絡來源數據）整合為統一的架構，旨在改善標籤的一致性和異質數據集之間的數據標準化。Surg$Σ$-DB涵蓋6個臨床專科和多樣的手術類型，提供18個實際手術任務的豐富影像和視頻級標註，涵蓋理解、推理、規劃和生成，規模前所未有（超過598萬次對話）。除了傳統的多模態對話外，Surg$Σ$-DB還納入了層次推理標註，提供更豐富的語義線索，以支持在複雜手術場景中的更深層次的上下文理解。我們進一步通過最近開發的基於Surg$Σ$-DB的手術基礎模型提供實證證據，說明大規模多模態標註、統一語義設計和結構化推理標註在改善跨任務泛化和可解釋性方面的實際好處。
2603.16822.author: Zhitao Zeng et.al.
2603.16822.authors: Zhitao Zeng, Mengya Xu, Jian Jiang, Pengfei Guo, Yunqiu Xu, Zhu Zhuo, Chang Han Low, Yufan He, Dong Yang, Chenxi Lin, Yiming Gu, Jiaxin Guo, Yutong Ban, Daguang Xu, Qi Dou, Yueming Jin
